title_en: The Legacy of Mizan al-I'tidal and the Continuation of Narrator Criticism
title_ar: أثر ميزان الاعتدال وامتداد نقد الرجال
content_en:
Mizan al-I'tidal became immediately authoritative in the hadith scholarship tradition and spawned a series of continuations, supplements, and critiques that extended its influence through the later classical period. Al-Dhahabi himself was aware that his work was necessarily incomplete and that subsequent scholars would need to correct his assessments in some cases.

Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani and Lisan al-Mizan

The most important continuation of Mizan al-I'tidal is Lisan al-Mizan (The Language of the Balance) by Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani (773–852 AH / 1372–1449 CE), arguably the greatest hadith scholar of the post-classical period. Ibn Hajar used al-Dhahabi's Mizan as his base text and added numerous entries that al-Dhahabi had omitted, corrected assessments he considered mistaken, and updated the work with biographical information that had become available after al-Dhahabi's death.

Ibn Hajar was willing to disagree with al-Dhahabi on specific narrators, and his Lisan al-Mizan contains a number of entries where he explicitly defends narrators whom al-Dhahabi had criticized or criticizes narrators whom al-Dhahabi had defended. This scholarly dialogue between the two greatest hadith critics of their respective centuries represents the hadith tradition at its most intellectually vital.

Al-Dhahabi's Self-Corrections

Al-Dhahabi was unusually willing among classical scholars to acknowledge his own errors and revise his judgments. Several entries in Mizan al-I'tidal include notes that he added after his initial composition, correcting or qualifying earlier assessments. In some cases he mentions being shown new information that changed his view; in others he reconsiders an initial harsh judgment in light of the overall body of evidence for a narrator.

This self-critical stance was admired by subsequent scholars as a model of scholarly integrity. It also reflects al-Dhahabi's understanding that narrator criticism is an enterprise that admits of degrees of certainty — some cases are clear beyond reasonable doubt (the confirmed fabricator, the narrator universally praised by reliable contemporaries), while others involve genuine uncertainty that honest scholars should acknowledge rather than paper over with false confidence.

The Enduring Relevance of Narrator Criticism

The science of narrator criticism that Mizan al-I'tidal exemplifies remains relevant to contemporary Islamic scholarship for several reasons. The hadith collections that Muslims use for legal and spiritual guidance today were built on the foundation of narrator evaluation — the decisions of critics like al-Dhahabi about which narrators to trust determined which hadiths entered the major collections and which were excluded.

Modern scholarship has also engaged with the hadith tradition critically, sometimes questioning whether the elaborate apparatus of narrator criticism was effective at preventing the transmission of unreliable material. Al-Dhahabi's Mizan al-I'tidal and the broader tradition it represents are central to these discussions — both as evidence of the sophisticated self-critical mechanisms within the hadith tradition and as objects of analysis for those who wish to understand how Islamic religious knowledge was constructed and maintained.

Al-Dhahabi himself, writing with the awareness of a scholar who had processed thousands of biographical entries, understood that the enterprise of narrator criticism was ultimately in service of a sacred goal: preserving the authentic transmission of the Prophet's teachings. His combination of scholarly rigor and genuine piety gives Mizan al-I'tidal its enduring authority as the most comprehensive Islamic evaluation of the human beings through whom the prophetic tradition was transmitted.
title_id: Warisan Mizan al-I'tidal dan Kelanjutan Kritik Perawi
content_id:
Mizan al-I'tidal segera menjadi otoritatif dalam tradisi keilmuan hadis dan melahirkan serangkaian kelanjutan, suplemen, dan kritik yang memperpanjang pengaruhnya melalui generasi-generasi ilmiah berikutnya. Karya al-Dzahabi adalah kompilasi paling komprehensif sebelum Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani memperluas dan merevisinya dalam Lisan al-Mizan, dan bahkan setelah perluasan Ibn Hajar, Mizan tetap dikonsultasikan karena penilaian-penilaian independen al-Dzahabi dan untuk kasus-kasus di mana al-Dzahabi tidak setuju dengan penilaian-penilaian selanjutnya dari Ibn Hajar.

Lisan al-Mizan karya Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani, ditulis sekitar satu abad setelah Mizan, merupakan respons ilmiah utama terhadap karya al-Dzahabi. Ibn Hajar memperluas cakupan Mizan untuk memasukkan perawi-perawi dari sumber-sumber yang lebih luas, mengoreksi kesalahan-kesalahan faktual, dan merevisi beberapa penilaian al-Dzahabi berdasarkan penelitiannya sendiri. Dua karya tersebut sekarang biasanya dikonsultasikan bersama-sama, dengan Lisan al-Mizan yang memberikan koreksi-koreksi dan perluasan-perluasan Ibn Hajar yang tak ternilai.

Bagi para pelajar kontemporer ilmu hadis, Mizan al-I'tidal tetap menjadi teks yang tidak dapat dihindari. Ini adalah tempat pertama untuk mengakses penilaian-penilaian komprehensif dari para kritikus hadis klasik tentang perawi-perawi yang dipertanyakan. Edisi-edisi ilmiah modern dengan penomoran entri yang sistematis dan indeks-indeks yang komprehensif telah menjadikannya lebih dapat diakses daripada yang pernah ada dalam tradisi manuskrip, dan pengenalan terhadapnya adalah bagian standar dari kurikulum ilmu hadis tingkat lanjut.
content_ar:
أحدث ميزان الاعتدال أثرًا بالغًا في مسار علم الجرح والتعديل من بعد الذهبي؛ إذ غدا المرجعَ الأساسي لكل من جاء بعده من أئمة هذا الفن.

ومن أبرز من أفاد من الميزان ابنُ حجر العسقلاني الذي أعدّ «لسان الميزان» تتمةً له، مضيفًا فيه رواةً ذكرهم الذهبي في مؤلفات أخرى أو فاتته تراجمهم في الميزان، مع تعقّبات ومستدركات. وتُشير «لسان الميزان» إلى كل موضع خالف فيه ابن حجر الذهبي أو استدرك عليه.

وكذلك استفاد من الميزان السخاوي في «الضوء اللامع» وابن العماد الحنبلي في «شذرات الذهب»، فضلًا عن عدد كبير من العلماء المتأخرين الذين جعلوه حجر الأساس في دراسة الرواة المنتقَدين.

وفي العصر الحديث يظل الميزان مرجعًا لا يُستغنى عنه في البحث الحديثي، ويُقتبس منه في رسائل الماجستير والدكتوراه المتعلقة بعلوم الحديث في أرجاء العالم الإسلامي. وتُعدّ طبعة علي محمد البجاوي الطبعةَ المعتمدة حتى اليوم.
